rules.hint: Domain layer: no references to Infrastructure, EF Core, or any framework package — pure C# entities, value objects, domain events only.
Application layer: define interfaces (ports) here; Infrastructure implements them. Prefer CQRS/MediatR handlers over fat service classes.
Infrastructure layer: implement Application interfaces. Use Repository/UnitOfWork for persistence; keep DbContext usage out of Domain/Application.
Controllers: thin — map request to a Mediator command/query and return the result; no business logic inline.
React component: presentation only — no direct API/data-layer calls or business rules; consume a hook or service for that.
Service layer: depend on interfaces/ports, not concrete implementations, so it stays testable and swappable (dependency inversion).
Prefer composition over inheritance; keep modules single-purpose (SRP) and depend on abstractions at module boundaries.
